title: Ray County Commissioner's Meeting 4/29
showNotes:
# County Commission Meeting Summary

## Opening Proceedings
The meeting began with an invocation prayer focusing on guidance for county officials and workers. Commissioners and attendees were wearing blue in recognition of Child Abuse Prevention Day, with a ceremony scheduled for 11:30 AM. Julie was recognized for her efforts in decorating the county grounds for the occasion.

## Public Comment Period

### Road Maintenance Concerns
A resident raised ongoing concerns about ditch maintenance on their road, specifically noting that county workers had been driving up and down the road but placed a ditch on the opposite side of where it was needed. The resident announced the launch of a new website, f3oads.com (where "f" stands for "fix"), designed to allow citizens to report road issues. The site will route complaints to appropriate special road districts and county email addresses, with an open map displaying all submitted issues.

Another resident at 1434 Aikenwood in Raymore reported severe drainage problems. Despite multiple rock applications on a hill, rain continuously washes the rock away, plugging the driveway pipe. The resident, a retired road worker with 30 years of experience, suggested that the county's approach of cutting the ditch was accelerating water flow rather than slowing it. The resident offered to purchase a new pipe if the county would install it.

### Sunshine Request Procedures
Discussion addressed changes to the Sunshine Request process, including new prepayment requirements for research costs. The commission confirmed that requests submitted prior to the rate change would be honored at the old rate. Residents with unfulfilled requests were advised to resubmit them. The current contact email address remains the same for now.

A citizen asked about establishing a pre-funded account for multiple requests rather than paying individually, but the commission declined due to accounting complications, requiring individual payments per request.

### County Road Complaint Form
Two citizens, Lindsay and another resident, presented a Google Doc form they had prepared for the county website to standardize road complaint submissions. The commission expressed preference for a county-initiated and county-controlled form rather than accepting submissions through external channels. The citizens' form will be reviewed for potential adoption as the official county document.

## Administrative Actions

### Minutes and Payables
The commission voted to untable and subsequently approve minutes from April 22nd and 23rd. Payables were also approved.

### Planning and Zoning
A plat for Tucker Razors was approved, with one commissioner recusing themselves from the vote due to a disclosed conflict of interest.

## Engineering Report - Great River Engineering

### Qualification-Based Selection Process
The engineering firm provided detailed information about selection processes for engineering consultants. Under the federal Brooks Act and Missouri's "mini-Brooks Act," qualification-based selection is required for projects using federal funds and recommended for local funds. For contracts under $200,000 in engineering fees, the county can select from MoDOT's pre-approved consultant list. Contracts exceeding $200,000 require a formal Request for Qualifications process. The firm noted that existing relationships with qualified firms can be continued without a new RFQ process.

### Myers Road Bridge Project
Design modifications have reduced the necessary grade increase from 6-8 feet to approximately 5 feet through the addition of a vertical curve on the bridge structure. The substructure design includes cast-in-place walls with pile footings extending 20-25 feet to bedrock due to unstable glacial till conditions. Temporary construction easements will likely be needed from four property owners: Joni Myers, Sharon Myers, Carl Winkle, and Mary Van Winkle. Final plans are expected to be submitted in June or July, with construction bidding to follow and actual construction beginning next spring.

### West 216th Street Bridge
This project will utilize precast box culverts rather than a traditional bridge structure. The design includes two 12-foot lanes with headwalls at the inlet and outlet. A contractor will be hired to set the precast boxes, as county forces lack the necessary equipment. The structure will fall within the existing 60-foot right-of-way corridor shown on county GIS maps, though the commission may want to secure formal easements or obtain property owner permission for access.

### West 84th Street Bridge
Survey work revealed that conditions are more severe than initially anticipated, with approximately 18 feet of depth requiring a structure with a bearing length of approximately 78.5 feet. The proposed design is a precast NU girder bridge. Construction cost estimates range from $500,000 to $600,000, with engineering design costs of $130,000. The firm provided examples of similar projects with bids ranging from $410,000 to $682,000, noting that construction bids can vary significantly based on contractor workload and market conditions.

### Lexington/Henrietta Bridge Demolition
The commission is pursuing demolition of the Lexington/Henrietta bridge structure. MoDOT allows demolition to count toward Bridge Replacement Off-system (BRO) program requirements, and the county may receive soft match credit for the demolition costs. Discussion centered on whether to remove only the superstructure (beams and deck) or the entire structure including substructure. The substructure appears stable and could potentially remain for decades, and partial demolition would significantly reduce costs. However, full removal involves complications with railroad track proximity requiring shoring and excavation support. The commission expressed interest in partial removal due to cost constraints.

### Upcoming BRO Applications
The next round of BRO applications opens April 1st. The commission needs to identify its top two priority bridges for application. Bridges qualify for the program when deck, superstructure, substructure, or culvert components are rated 4 or below (poor condition) on a 1-10 scale based on biennial inspections.

## Bid Waiver - Holt College
The commission approved a motion to waive irregularity regarding a single bid submission for the Holt College project that was received one hour past the deadline. The county attorney, Travis Elliott, advised that the risk was minimal given there was only one bidder. The motion passed unanimously to review the bid for consideration.

## Announcements and Future Business
The Eagleton Agreement regarding rental changes will be discussed at the following week's meeting. The token agenda item was postponed to the following week. A commissioner noted an upcoming commitment at 10:00 AM.
shortSummary:
The county commission meeting began with a proclamation for Child Abuse Prevention Day and public comments regarding road drainage issues. A resident introduced a third-party website for reporting road problems, though commissioners emphasized the need for an official county-run form. 

During administrative business, the board approved a plat for Tucker Razors and voted to waive an irregularity for a late bid regarding Holt College, allowing it to be reviewed. An engineering firm provided updates on several bridge projects, including Myers Road, West 216, and West 84th Street, discussing design changes, qualification-based selection processes, and construction estimates. The meeting concluded with plans to discuss the Eagleton Agreement next week.
